---
title: "data_analysis_2"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
1 + 1
```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
2 * 2
```

The `echo: false` option disables the printing of code (only output is displayed).

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(corrplot)
library(GGally)
df=read.csv("C:/Users/3165152Z/Downloads/dataset11.csv")

```

```{r}
colSums(is.na(df))
```

```{r}
# omit the NA
coffee<- na.omit(df)

```

```{r}
# some data have extremely high altitude which i suppose to be mistake
coffee=coffee[coffee$altitude_mean_meters<5000,]
```

```{r}
pairs(coffee[,c(2,3,4,5,6)],pch=20,lower.panel = NULL)
```

```{r}
# Factorize
coffee$Qualityclass <- as.factor(coffee$Qualityclass)
coffee$Qualityclass <- factor(coffee$Qualityclass, levels = c("Poor", "Good"))
```

```{r}
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = aroma, fill = Qualityclass)) +
     geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity", bins = 20) +
     theme_minimal()
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = flavor, fill = Qualityclass)) +
     geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity", bins = 20) +
     theme_minimal()
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = acidity, fill = Qualityclass)) +
     geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity", bins = 20) +
     theme_minimal()
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = category_two_defects, fill = Qualityclass)) +
     geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity", bins = 20) +
     theme_minimal()
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = altitude_mean_meters, fill = Qualityclass)) +
     geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity", bins = 20) +
     theme_minimal()
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = harvested, fill = Qualityclass)) +
     geom_histogram(alpha = 0.6, position = "identity", bins = 20) +
     theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = Qualityclass, y = aroma)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Qualityclass", y = "aroma",
         title = "Qualityclass by aroma")
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = Qualityclass, y = flavor)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Qualityclass", y = "flavor",
         title = "Qualityclass by flavor")
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = Qualityclass, y = acidity)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Qualityclass", y = "acidity",
         title = "Qualityclass by acidity")
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = Qualityclass, y = category_two_defects)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Qualityclass", y = "category_two_defects",
         title = "Qualityclass by category_two_defects")
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = Qualityclass, y = altitude_mean_meters)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Qualityclass", y = "altitude_mean_meters",
         title = "Qualityclass by altitude_mean_meters")
ggplot(coffee, aes(x = Qualityclass, y = harvested)) +
    geom_boxplot() +
    labs(x = "Qualityclass", y = "harvested",
         title = "Qualityclass by harvested")
```

```{r}
library(car)
model <- glm(Qualityclass ~ aroma + flavor+acidity + category_two_defects +
             altitude_mean_meters + harvested,
             data = coffee, family = binomial(link = "logit"))
vif(model)
summary(model)
```

```{r}
# stepwise selecting model via AIC
null_model <- glm(Qualityclass ~ 1,
                  data = coffee,
                  family = binomial)
step_model <- step(null_model, 
                   scope = list(lower = null_model, upper = model),
                   direction = "both",
                   trace = TRUE)
summary(step_model)
```

```{r}
library(broom)
library(kableExtra)

tidy(step_model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) |>
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), \(x) round(x, 3))) |>
  kbl(col.names = c("Term", "OR", "SE", "z", "p", "CI low", "CI high"),
      caption = "Logistic regression -- odds ratios with 95% CIs") |>
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed"))
```

```{r}
library(sjPlot)

# Coefficient plot with CIs
plot_model(step_model, type = "est", show.values = TRUE)
```
